claim: For all integers n > 1, sigma(n) is at least n + 1, with equality exactly when n is prime.

claim: If n is a product of two distinct primes p and q, then sigma(n) >= n + p + q + 1.
predicate: (not (is_prime(n) or len([p for p in range(2, int(n**0.5)+1) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p) and is_prime(n // p) and p != n // p]) == 0)) or (sigma(n) >= n + [p for p in range(2, int(n**0.5)+1) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p) and is_prime(n // p) and p != n // p][0] + [p for p in range(2, int(n**0.5)+1) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p) and is_prime(n // p) and p != n // p][1] + 1)

claim: For all n > 1, if sigma(n) = n + 2, then n is either a square of a prime or a product of two distinct primes.

predicate: (not (is_prime(n) or len([p for p in range(2, int(n**0.5)+1) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p) and is_prime(n // p) and p != n // p]) == 0)) or ((sigma(n) - n - 1) % gcd([p for p in range(2, int(n**0.5)+1) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p) and is_prime(n // p) and p != n // p][0] + 1, [p for p in range(2, int(n**0.5)+1) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p) and is_prime(n // p) and p != n // p][1] + 1) == 0)

claim: For all n > 1, if n is even and sigma(n) = n + 2, then n is twice a prime.
predicate: (n <= 1 or n % 2 != 0) or (sigma(n) != n + 2) or (is_prime(n // 2))

claim: For all n > 1, if n is twice a prime and n > 4, then sigma(n) = n + 2 + (n / 2).
predicate: (n <= 1) or (not (n % 2 == 0 and is_prime(n // 2) and n > 4)) or (sigma(n) == n + 2 + (n // 2))

claim: For all n > 1, if sigma(n) = n + 3, then n is a square of a prime.
predicate: (n <= 1) or (sigma(n) != n + 3) or (any(p*p == n and is_prime(p) for p in range(2, int(n**0.5)+1)))

predicate: (n <= 1) or (not any(2**k == n for k in range(2, int(math.log(n, 2))+1))) or (sigma(n) % 2 == 1)

claim: For all n > 1, if sigma(n) is odd, then n is a square or twice a square.
